a.description: Activity intensity (meetings + emails 30d) — inverted so low activity = high neglect

b.description: Meeting acceleration (7d vs 30d) — inverted so declining meetings = high neglect

c.description: Email responsiveness — inverted so one-way email = high neglect

description: Composite score measuring how neglected an account is. INVERTED scale — higher scores indicate MORE neglect. Combines low activity intensity, declining meeting momentum, and poor email responsiveness. Designed to surface high-value accounts that are quietly going dark.
coaching_text: Above 0.70 = Critical neglect — high-value account with near-zero engagement, immediate outreach required. 0.40-0.70 = Watch list — engagement declining, schedule proactive check-in. Below 0.40 = Covered — account has adequate recent activity.

_meta.note: This metric uses inverted scoring (1/(x+1)) so that LOW activity produces HIGH neglect scores. Sort descending to find most neglected accounts.
